cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So Jennifer went to the swimming pool today, and we need to find out between what times she could have gone. The options are A, B, C, D. Let me go through each of the given information points step by step. First, Jennifer woke up at 5am. Then Richard saw her working at the office from 5am to 8am. So from 5 to 8, she's at the office. Then Samantha saw her reading at the library from 12pm to 1pm. So 12 to 1, she's at the library. Then Betty saw her buying a bike at the bike shop from 1pm to 2pm. So that's 1 to 2. Then Sarah saw her buying a phone at the electronics store from 2pm to 9pm. Wait, that's a long time—2pm to 9pm. And then Thomas saw her walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. Also, the swimming pool was closed after 10pm. So the question is, when could she have gone to the swimming pool? She needs to go when she's not doing any of these other activities, and before the pool closes at 10pm. Let me outline her schedule: - 5am to 8am: Office (Richard) - 8am to 12pm: What? After 8am, there's nothing mentioned until 12pm. So maybe she's free between 8am and 12pm? Then from 12pm to 1pm: Library (Samantha) - 1pm to 2pm: Bike shop (Betty) - 2pm to 9pm: Electronics store (Sarah) - 9pm to 10pm: Walking to Statue of Liberty (Thomas) - After 10pm: Pool closed. So the possible free time is between when she finishes at the office (8am) until she goes to the library at 12pm. Then after that, she's occupied again. So between 8am and 12pm, she might have been at the pool. Let me check the options. Option A is 8am to 12pm. That seems possible. But let's check the other options. Option B is 9pm to 10pm. But during that time, she's walking towards the Statue of Liberty. So she can't be at both places. So B is out. Option C is 2pm to 9pm, but during that time she's at the electronics store. So she can't go to the pool then. Option D is 12pm to 1pm, when she's at the library. So
